  • Tutorial: Heating Systems & Pipe Networks

Tutorial: Heating Systems & Pipe Networks

GreenhousesJL models realistic heating systems matching the Greenhouses Modelica Library HVAC components (CHP.mo, HeatPump.mo, HeatingPipe.mo).

Architecture

The heating chain is: PI controller $\to$ Heat generator $\to$ Pipe network $\to$ Air

Q_demand (PI)  →  Generator (η/COP)  →  Water in pipes (C_pipe)  →  Convection + Radiation  →  Air
                  fuel consumed           thermal inertia            position-dependent

Heat generators

PresetTypeEfficiencyFuelSource
DirectHeating()Electric resistance$\eta = 1.0$Electricity—
Boiler()Gas boiler$\eta = 0.92$GasCHP.mo
CondensingBoiler()Condensing$\eta = 0.97$Gas—
AirSourceHP()Heat pump (air)COP = 3–5ElectricityHeatPump.mo
GroundSourceHP()Heat pump (ground)COP = 4.5–5.5ElectricityHeatPump.mo
GasCHP()Cogeneration$\eta_{th} = 0.50$GasCHP.mo

Heat pump COP model (Greenhouses Library)

\[COP = \eta_{II} \times \frac{T_{supply}}{T_{supply} - T_{source}}\]

where $\eta_{II} = 0.40$ (second-law efficiency), $T_{supply} = 308.15$ K (35 °C for floor heating).

For air-source: $T_{source} = T_{outdoor}$. For ground-source: $T_{source} = 283.15$ K (10 °C).

Source: Greenhouses Library HeatPump.mo (Altes-Buch et al. 2019)

Pipe network positions

The pipe position in the greenhouse determines convection regime and radiation view factors.

PresetPositionDiameterDensityConvectionSource
StandardPipes()Overhead (:high)DN511.5 m/m²FreeBalemans (1989)
GrowthPipes()Under canopy (:low)DN511.5 m/m²HinderedBot (1983)
RailPipes()Rail near floor (:low)DN510.8 m/m²HinderedBot (1983)
UnderBenchPipes()Under bench (:under_bench)DN352.0 m/m²HinderedBot (1983)
FloorHeatingPipes()Embedded (:floor)DN253.0 m/m²ConductionIncropera

Convection correlations (PipeFreeConvection.mo)

Free pipe (overhead, in open air), Balemans (1989):

\[\alpha = 1.28 \times d^{-0.25} \times |\Delta T|^{0.25} \quad [W/(m^2_{pipe} \cdot K)]\]

Hindered pipe (near floor/canopy/bench), Bot (1983):

\[\alpha = 1.99 \times |\Delta T|^{0.32} \quad [W/(m^2_{pipe} \cdot K)]\]

The hindered correlation is diameter-independent and gives slightly lower values for small $\Delta T$ but higher for large $\Delta T$.

View factor (HeatingPipe.mo)

\[FF = \frac{N_p \times \pi \times d \times l}{A} \times c\]

where $c = 0.50$ (free) or $c = 0.49$ (hindered), from HeatingPipe.mo lines 54–60.

Radiation with intermediate obstruction (Radiation_T4.mo)

\[Q_{rad} = A \times \varepsilon_a \varepsilon_b \times FF_a \times FF_b \times \prod_{k} (1 - FF_{k}) \times \sigma (T_a^4 - T_b^4)\]

Each intermediate surface (canopy, screen, other pipe network) reduces the view factor by $(1 - FF_k)$.

Pipe emissivity: $\varepsilon_{pipe} = 0.88$ (steel pipe with paint)

Pipe thermal inertia

The water mass in the pipes creates a first-order thermal lag:

\[C_{pipe} \frac{dT_{pipe}}{dt} = Q_{supply} - UA_{pipe} \times (T_{pipe} - T_{air})\]

Position$UA$ [W/(m²·K)]$\tau$ [min]Behaviour
Overhead2.9273Slow response, overshoot
Under canopy2.8276Similar to overhead
Rail1.5176Low density = lower UA
Under bench2.5852DN35, faster response
Floor4.0925Fast, direct conduction

Example: Boiler with overhead pipes

using GreenhousesJL

tmy = load_tmy("path/to/10Dec-22Nov.txt")

p = GreenhouseParams(tmy;
    A     = 14000.0,
    Kp    = 100.0 * 14000,
    Ti    = 300.0,
    T_sp  = 289.15,
    Q_max = 200.0 * 14000,
    heating = HeatingConfig(Boiler(), StandardPipes()),
)
sol = solve(ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, initial_state(p), (0.0, 7*86400.0), p),
    Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)

Example: Heat pump with under-bench pipes (soilless)

p = GreenhouseParams(tmy;
    A     = 200.0,
    Kp    = 100.0 * 200,
    Ti    = 300.0,
    T_sp  = 291.15,        # 18 °C
    Q_max = 200.0 * 200,
    heating = HeatingConfig(AirSourceHP(Q_nom=40000.0), UnderBenchPipes()),
    crop  = LettuceCrop(),
)

CO2 emission factors

FuelFactor [kgCO2/kWh]Note
Electricity (France)0.060Nuclear-dominated mix
Natural gas0.205Combustion
Fuel oil0.270Combustion
Biogas0.020Near-zero (upstream only)

For CHP, the co-produced electricity offsets grid emissions: $CO2_{credit} = E_{elec} \times 0.060$.

EnergyPlus benchmark results

Validated on 14,000 m² greenhouse, Brussels Dec 10–17:

SystemEP E_heatJL E_heat$\Delta E$Speedup
Direct6.68 kWh/m²7.14 kWh/m²+7%35×
Boiler7.807.30−6%24×
Heat pump7.807.51−4%17×
CHP7.807.30−6%24×
